title: DevOps & CI/CD Foundation — Repo, Build, Containerisation & Local Orchestration
problem_statement: BrokerEdge is a greenfield system with no source repository yet, composed of multiple independently buildable services (REST API, Batch, UI, and a Node.js Mock Service) that must compile, containerise, and run together end-to-end on a single 16 GB developer machine. There is currently no version-controlled monorepo, no reproducible multi-module build, no container packaging, no continuous-integration gate, and no one-command local environment. Until this foundation exists, no feature epic can be implemented, integrated, or verified, and every downstream agent lacks a repository and running target to build against.

goals.goal: Establish a reproducible multi-module Maven build for all backend services.
goals.success_measure: A clean checkout builds all backend modules with a single `mvn clean install` in under 5 minutes with zero manual steps.
goals.measurement_method: CI build log timing and process exit code.

goals.goal: Provide a one-command local orchestration stack that fits the 16 GB constraint.
goals.success_measure: `docker compose up` starts api-service, batch-service, PostgreSQL, Kafka, Schema Registry, Mock Service, and the UI with total resident memory of the container stack at or below 2 GB, and all container health checks report healthy within 120 seconds.
goals.measurement_method: `docker stats` memory readings and `docker compose ps` health status captured after startup.

goals.goal: Enforce an automated CI quality gate on every change.
goals.success_measure: Every pull request runs lint, unit tests, and produces a build artifact; a failing stage marks the PR's required status check as failed so it is not merged (merge is gated by required-check policy, since a free-plan private repo cannot enforce server-side branch protection).
goals.measurement_method: Jenkins pipeline stage results and the PR commit status-check state.

goals.goal: Bootstrap the private GitHub monorepo that all epics build in.
goals.success_measure: A single private monorepo exists under the configured owner with the standard directory layout, the initial scaffold pushed to the default branch, and a documented branch-per-story workflow; re-running bootstrap does not create a duplicate repo.
goals.measurement_method: GitHub API confirms the repo exists and is private, contains the scaffold on the default branch, and a second bootstrap run is a no-op.

personas.name: Sam
personas.role: BrokerEdge Engineer (full-stack developer / pipeline developer agent)
personas.goal: Build, test, run, and integrate the application on a reproducible pipeline, in a single monorepo, on a local environment.
personas.pain_point: Without a repository, a standard build, and a runnable local stack, every feature attempt starts from an inconsistent, non-reproducible baseline.

functional_requirements.description: Provide a Maven multi-module project: a parent POM (`brokerEdge-parent`) aggregating `api-service` and `batch-service` modules, with the `mock-service` (Node) and `ui` (React) maintained as separate toolchains within the same monorepo. Dependency versions and plugin management are centralised in the parent POM per Section 15.5.

Produce OCI container images for api-service and batch-service using the Spring Boot / buildpacks (`spring-boot:build-image`) approach, and a container image for the Mock Service and the Nginx-served UI.

Provide a Docker Compose stack defining every service (api, batch, postgres, kafka in KRaft mode, schema-registry, mock-service, ui) with a per-container `mem_limit`, health checks, and correct startup dependency ordering per Section 7.2.

Provide a Jenkinsfile implementing the pipeline stages defined in Section 9: PR validation (lint, unit tests, dependency scan), integration test, and build & package, reporting results as PR commit status checks.
Provide a runnable Spring Boot application skeleton for api-service and batch-service, each exposing an Actuator health endpoint and wired for Flyway migration execution on startup.
Provide a Helm chart stub targeting the Phase 2 Kubernetes (EKS) deployment, containing templated Deployments and Services for api-service and batch-service.
Enforce that no secret value is committed to source control: provide `.gitignore` entries for env and local config files and a CI secret-scan step per Section 16 AVOID-4.

Idempotently create the private GitHub monorepo (`${GITHUB_OWNER}/${GITHUB_REPO}`, resolved from run-config) using a classic Personal Access Token (`GITHUB_TOKEN`) with `repo` scope: create the repo if it does not exist, initialise the standard directory layout (`/api-service`, `/batch-service`, `/mock-service`, `/ui`, `/deploy`, `/docs`, root `pom.xml`, `.gitignore`, `README`), and push the initial scaffold to the default branch `main`. This supports both contributor models with no code change: in shared mode the repo already exists (contributors have push access) so creation is skipped and the existing repo is used; in own mode the contributor's `${GITHUB_OWNER}` does not yet have the repo so it is created. Because a free-plan private repo cannot enforce server-side branch protection, document and rely on the required-CI-check + branch-per-story convention instead of protected branches.

Commit the BrokerEdge design document into the monorepo at `/docs/BrokerEdge_Design_Document.md` as part of the initial scaffold, so every downstream agent can read the sections referenced by all epics (Section 6.2, 15.x, 16.x) from within a clone.

Configure Flyway with out-of-order migrations enabled in the local, dev, and ci profiles, because epics are ingested in a different order than their migration version numbers (e.g. BE-7 owns V009 but is ingested before BE-1 which owns V001); CI provisions a fresh database per run. Per Sections 6.2 and 15.6.

performance: `mvn clean install` for all backend modules completes in under 5 minutes on the reference 16 GB machine.
`docker compose up` reaches all-healthy state within 120 seconds.
scalability: Total container stack resident memory does not exceed 2 GB, leaving headroom for OS, IDE, and a Playwright browser on a 16 GB machine.
Each container declares an explicit `mem_limit` per the Section 7.2 memory budget.
availability: Every service container exposes a health check that Docker Compose uses to gate dependent service startup.
observability: api-service and batch-service expose `/actuator/health` and `/actuator/info` per Section 15.6.
security: No secret (JWT_SECRET, DB_PASSWORD, API keys, or the GitHub PAT) is present in any committed file; the PAT is supplied only via a pipeline credential/environment variable.
The repository is created as private.
CI includes a secret-scanning step that fails the build on detected secrets.

acceptance_criteria.scenario: Clean multi-module build succeeds from a fresh checkout
acceptance_criteria.given: a clean checkout of the repository on the reference machine with Java 21 and Maven 3.9 installed
acceptance_criteria.when: the engineer runs `mvn clean install` from the repository root
then: all backend modules compile and their unit tests pass
the build exits with code 0 in under 5 minutes
and: api-service and batch-service each produce a runnable Spring Boot jar

acceptance_criteria.scenario: Full stack starts locally within the memory budget
acceptance_criteria.given: built container images for all services
then: all containers reach a healthy state within 120 seconds
total container resident memory is at or below 2 GB
and: postgres, kafka, and schema-registry become healthy before api-service and batch-service start

acceptance_criteria.scenario: Service skeletons expose a green health endpoint
acceptance_criteria.when: an HTTP GET is issued to `/actuator/health` on api-service and on batch-service
and: Flyway reports the baseline migration state as current on startup

acceptance_criteria.scenario: CI fails the required check on a failing pull request
acceptance_criteria.given: a pull request that introduces a failing unit test
acceptance_criteria.when: the Jenkins pipeline runs against the pull request
then: the PR's required CI status check is reported as failed and the branch-per-story policy directs that it must not be merged

acceptance_criteria.given: a change that introduces a hardcoded secret value in a config file
then: the build fails with a message identifying the offending file

acceptance_criteria.scenario: Monorepo is created idempotently and privately
acceptance_criteria.given: a valid GitHub PAT with `repo` scope and a configured ${GITHUB_OWNER}
then: a private repo ${GITHUB_OWNER}/${GITHUB_REPO} exists with the standard layout and scaffold on `main`
and: running bootstrap again does not create a duplicate repo or overwrite existing history

in_scope: Idempotent creation of the private GitHub monorepo and initial scaffold push (default branch `main`)
Standard monorepo directory layout (/api-service, /batch-service, /mock-service, /ui, /deploy, root pom.xml)
Branch-per-story workflow documentation (required-CI-check gating in lieu of server-side branch protection)
Maven parent POM and api-service / batch-service module POMs with centralised dependency management
Container images for api-service, batch-service, mock-service, and the Nginx-served UI
Docker Compose stack with per-container memory limits, health checks, and startup ordering
Jenkinsfile implementing PR validation, integration, and build/package stages reported as PR status checks
Runnable Spring Boot skeletons with Actuator health and Flyway baseline wiring
Helm chart stub for the Phase 2 Kubernetes target
Source-control secret hygiene (.gitignore + CI secret scan)

out_of_scope.rationale: A free-plan personal private repo cannot enforce branch protection; the pipeline relies on required CI status checks and the branch-per-story convention instead.
A single monorepo is used per Section 11 Source Control Strategy; multi-repo is out of scope for v1.
v1 targets local Docker Compose; the Helm chart is a stub only per Section 13.

out_of_scope.item: Business logic, domain entities, and use cases
out_of_scope.rationale: Delivered by the domain epics BE-7, BE-1, BE-2, BE-5, BE-3, BE-6, BE-4.

out_of_scope.item: Secrets manager integration (Vault / AWS Secrets Manager)
out_of_scope.rationale: v1 injects secrets as environment variables; a secrets manager is a Phase 2 hardening item per Section 16 AVOID-4.
Delivered by BE-12 Testing Infrastructure, which this epic precedes.

tech_stack.source_control_auth: Classic Personal Access Token with `repo` scope

existing_patterns: Single monorepo containing all services and deploy assets (Section 11 Source Control Strategy)
Modular monolith (Phase 1) with Clean Architecture layering
Centralised Maven dependency and plugin management in the parent POM (Section 15.5)
Branch-per-story short-lived branches with slug-prefixed names (e.g. be-3/place-market-order)
integration_points: GitHub REST API for repository creation and scaffold push (PAT-authenticated)
Docker Compose service network wiring across api, batch, postgres, kafka, schema-registry, mock-service, ui
Actuator health endpoints consumed by Docker Compose health checks
deployment_constraints: Entire stack must run on a single machine with at least 16 GB RAM
Total container stack memory budgeted at approximately 2 GB with explicit per-container mem_limit
Kafka runs in KRaft mode to avoid a separate ZooKeeper container
Free-plan private repo: no server-side branch protection; enforce quality via required CI checks

github_repos.notes: Private monorepo created and scaffolded by this epic (FR-8). ${GITHUB_OWNER} and ${GITHUB_REPO} are resolved from the run-config (defaults TimLafleur-w/brokeredge); each contributor may point at the shared main repo or their own. Root contains the Maven parent POM plus /api-service, /batch-service, /mock-service, /ui, /docs, and /deploy. All later epics work in a subtree of this repo.

spec_documents.label: BrokerEdge Design Document — Section 7.2 (Local Docker Compose Stack), Section 9 (CI/CD), Section 11 (Source Control Strategy), Section 15.5 (Maven Multi-Module Structure), Section 16 (Antipatterns)
spec_documents.notes: Authoritative source for the build structure, memory budget, port mapping, source-control strategy, and pipeline stages.

risks.description: The combined container stack exceeds the 2 GB memory budget on the 16 GB reference machine.
risks.mitigation: Set explicit `mem_limit` per container per Section 7.2; tune JVM `-XX:MaxRAMPercentage` for api and batch; prefer Alpine base images.
risks.trigger: `docker stats` shows aggregate memory above 2 GB after `docker compose up`.
risks.contingency: Reduce JVM heap ceilings, disable non-essential services in a `dev` compose profile, and document a minimal-profile startup.

risks.description: Kafka in KRaft mode or Schema Registry fails to become healthy in time, blocking dependent service startup.
risks.mitigation: Add generous health-check start periods and retries; pin Kafka and Schema Registry image versions.
risks.trigger: api-service or batch-service containers restart-loop waiting on Kafka dependency health.
risks.contingency: Increase health-check `start_period`; add an explicit readiness wait script before dependent services start.

risks.description: Spring Boot buildpack image builds are slow or produce oversized images, hurting the build-time goal.
risks.mitigation: Enable buildpack layer caching in CI; use a slim JRE base image.
risks.trigger: Image build stage exceeds its time budget or images exceed a reasonable size threshold.
risks.contingency: Switch the affected module to a hand-written multi-stage Dockerfile.

risks.description: Free-plan private repo cannot enforce branch protection, so a failing PR could be merged manually, bypassing the quality gate.
risks.mitigation: Make required CI status checks the merge policy; document the branch-per-story workflow; keep PRs small and agent-gated.
risks.trigger: A merge lands on `main` with a failed or missing CI status check.
risks.contingency: Add a CI job that reverts or flags commits to `main` lacking a passing check, or upgrade to GitHub Pro to enable branch protection.

risks.description: The GitHub PAT is missing, under-scoped, or leaked, blocking repo bootstrap or exposing the account.
risks.mitigation: Require a `repo`-scoped classic PAT supplied only via a pipeline credential; never commit it; scope it to the single account.
risks.trigger: Repo creation fails with an authorization error, or the secret scan detects a token.
risks.contingency: Rotate the PAT immediately; re-run bootstrap with a correctly scoped token.

external.description: GitHub personal account (free plan) and a classic Personal Access Token with `repo` scope, supplied to the pipeline as a credential, used to create the private monorepo and push the scaffold.
Docker Desktop (or a compatible container runtime) installed and running on the developer machine.
JDK 21 and Maven 3.9 available on the build machine and CI agent.

definition_of_done: A private GitHub monorepo exists with the standard layout and initial scaffold, created idempotently by the pipeline.
A developer can clone the repository and run the entire application stack locally with a single command.
Every service exposes a health endpoint that reports healthy in the running local stack.
The CI pipeline runs on every pull request and reports a required status check that gates merges under the branch-per-story policy.
The container stack runs within the documented memory budget on a 16 GB machine.
No secret values exist anywhere in the source repository.

story_outline.title: Bootstrap private GitHub monorepo and scaffold
Scaffold Maven parent POM and service modules
Create runnable Spring Boot skeletons with Actuator and Flyway wiring
Build container images for all services
Author Docker Compose stack with memory limits and health checks
Implement Jenkinsfile CI pipeline with PR status checks
